title: verbascum pubescens
intro: Verbascum pubescens, also known as silky mullein or woolly mullein, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall spikes of yellow flowers and soft, silvery-gray leaves. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with the leaves and flowers used to create medicinal preparations. The health benefits of Verbascum pubescens include its ability to soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Verbascum pubescens can be made into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, with the dried leaves and flowers often added to hot water or oil for topical applications.
